summary: iOS 👀 | 5200 | 4320 | DFU is NOT blocked during the call
scenario: DFU firmware update is not blocked during an active call on Device Settings > Software screen
description_clean: DFU (firmware update) is not blocked during an active call. When a user is on a call and navigates to Device Settings > Software to tap Update, they are taken to the Software Update screen and allowed to trigger DFU. Expected behavior: a popup message should inform the user that DFU is not available during a call.
steps: 1. Launch Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Connect to a Poly Bluetooth audio device.
3. Make or receive an active call using the connected device.
4. While on the active call, navigate to Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings.
5. Tap on 'Software' in the Device Settings menu.
6. Observe that the Software Update screen is displayed.
7. Tap the 'Update' button to trigger DFU (firmware update).
expected: A popup message should be displayed informing the user that DFU (firmware update) is not available during an active call, and the update should be blocked.
actual: No popup is shown. The user is taken to the Software Update screen and allowed to trigger DFU while still on the active call.
